D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Gray World vs Retinex vs MaxRGB

three automatic white-balance algorithms agree on neutral scenes and disagree as soon as a single colour dominates the frame

Automatic white balance estimates a scene gain (r,g,b) so that the corrected image looks neutral under canonical light. Three classic estimators: Gray World assumes the average of all pixels is gray; MaxRGB assumes the brightest pixel is the illuminant; Retinex assumes each channel's robust maximum is the illuminant. They give the same answer on a balanced scene — and very different answers when one colour dominates.

The scene is a Macbeth chart under a warm cast. Drag the dominant-colour slider to add a coloured cloth that floods the frame. Switch algorithm and watch the corrected chart: Gray World fails first (its mean shifts with the cloth), MaxRGB drifts as a saturated swatch saturates the maximum, Retinex is more robust but still biases.
